keyPoints: The primary goal of software engineering is to develop high-quality software on time and within budget.
The Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC) provides a structured process for building systems.
Project Management is crucial for planning, monitoring, and controlling resources to achieve project goals.
commonMistakes: Scope Creep: Adding new features after the project scope has been defined, without adjusting timeline or budget.
Ignoring Non-Functional Requirements: Focusing only on what the software does (features) and not on how it performs (security, scalability, usability).
Poor Risk Management: Failing to identify potential problems early and not having mitigation plans in place.
